topic.what: Horizontal single-trace SCA attacks on binary kP algorithms using Chevallier-Mames atomic block patterns
topic.how: Using EM emanation measurements and Pearson correlation template matching to distinguish atomic blocks and extract scalar bits
topic.why_matters: Demonstrates that atomic block countermeasures are insufficient against SCA attacks even with projective coordinate randomization, revealing persistent attack surface in EC implementations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Extraction of scalar bits from single EM traces
characteristics.evidence: Successfully distinguished PD and PA operations to extract scalar bits
characteristics.context: Microcontroller and FPGA implementations using Chevallier-Mames atomic patterns

characteristics.what_it_measures: Visual similarity of atomic block shapes in EM traces
characteristics.evidence: Shapes of atomic blocks Δ1-Δ4 and Δ9 in Q+P differ significantly from other blocks
characteristics.context: Field multiplication with small operands (0,1,-3) causes distinguishable energy patterns

characteristics.what_itasures: Pearson correlation between atomic block templates and trace segments
characteristics.evidence: Atoms Δ1,Δ2,Δ4,Δ7 in PD show high correlation with template (>0.9) while atoms in PA do not
characteristics.context: Template matching enables distinguishing PD from PA operations

characteristics.vs_baseline: atomicity aims to prevent such leakage
characteristics.evidence: Multiplication of special small operands (0,1,-3) consumes less energy than processing full 64-bit operands

characteristics.vs_baseline: projective coordinate randomization does not prevent this
characteristics.evidence: Different variables and data-flow paths lead to different address accesses causing SCA leakage at start of field operations
characteristics.context: Left-to-right kP with projective coordinate randomization

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability of countermeasures to prevent attacks
characteristics.evidence: Projective coordinate randomization prevents data-bit leakage but not address-bit leakage

novelty: First demonstration of Chevallier-Mames atomic block vulnerability to single-trace SCA
Analysis of address-bit leakage persisting after projective coordinate randomization
Template-based approach using Pearson correlation for atomic block identification
Application of data-bit and address-bit leakage analysis to left-to-right kP with randomization
open_problems: Deep investigation of address-bit leakage countermeasures for embedded processors
Investigation of atomic patterns from other works for improved attack results
Evaluation of field addition as potential SCA leakage source
Development of effective countermeasures against address-bit leakage in atomic patterns
